description:
You are acting as an “Artifact-Maintenance Bot” for a small binary-repository that lives entirely in the normal user’s HOME.  
    The repository already contains two top level collections – “stable” and “testing”.  
    From time to time every “*-SNAPSHOT.jar” file has to be removed from those collections and parked in a separate folder named “deprecated” so that the nightly build system stops downloading them.  
    
    Your goal is to perform that clean-up exclusively with standard command–line utilities, using the classic “find … | xargs …” (or any of its safe variants) pattern.  Do **NOT** use any GUI programs or text editors – this is 100 % terminal work.

    Initial lay-out (already present when you start):

    /home/user/artifacts/
    ├── stable/
    │   ├── library-1.0.jar          (binary – keep)
    │   └── library-1.1-SNAPSHOT.jar (binary – must be moved)
    └── testing/
        ├── tool-2.0.jar             (binary – keep)
        └── tool-2.0-SNAPSHOT.jar    (binary – must be moved)

    Tasks to accomplish (use **5-10 commands** in total):

    1. Inside /home/user/artifacts **create** a directory called “deprecated”.  
       • Only create it if it does not yet exist.  
    2. Use find and xargs (or find … -exec) to locate every file that  
       a) lives anywhere below “stable” or “testing”, and  
       b) has a name that matches the glob “*-SNAPSHOT.jar”.  
    3. Move each of those files into /home/user/artifacts/deprecated/ (all in the same directory, no sub-directories).  
    4. For every single file you move, compute its SHA-256 checksum *after* it has been moved.  
    5. Append one line per moved file to the log file  
       /home/user/artifacts/deprecation.log  
       in the exact format shown below (the automated tests will parse it byte-for-byte):  
    
       YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ <space> ORIGINAL_PATH <space> -> <space> NEW_PATH <space> SHA256=<64-hex-chars>\n
       
       Example (for illustration – **do not hard-code values**):
       2025-01-30T14:05:02Z /home/user/artifacts/stable/example-0.1-SNAPSHOT.jar -> /home/user/artifacts/deprecated/example-0.1-SNAPSHOT.jar SHA256=0123abcd…

       • The timestamp must be in UTC and in ISO-8601 basic format “YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ”.  
       • ORIGINAL_PATH is the absolute path *before* the move.  
       • NEW_PATH is the absolute path *after* the move (inside “deprecated”).  
       • SHA256 must be the real hash of the *moved* file and has to consist of exactly 64 lowercase hexadecimal characters.  
       • Each log entry ends with a single “\n”.  
    6. Make sure that:  
       • Both release jars remain in their original directories.  
       • The two snapshot jars end up inside /home/user/artifacts/deprecated.  
       • /home/user/artifacts/deprecation.log exists and contains exactly two well-formed lines, one for each moved jar, in any order.

    Constraints & Hints:

    • Work only in /home/user – you do **not** have sudo rights.  
    • Use at most 10 shell commands in total for the whole job.  
    • Defensive scripting matters: protect paths with spaces by using “-print0 | xargs -0” or find’s “-exec … {} +” variant.  
    • Remember that “sha256sum” prints “<hash>  <filename>” – you will need to strip everything except the first field for the log.  
    • Touching the log file more than once is OK as long as the final content matches the required format.  
    • No additional output files are allowed; the only artifact you create must be /home/user/artifacts/deprecation.log.

    When you are done, you should be able to run:

      ls /home/user/artifacts/deprecated

    and see exactly:

      library-1.1-SNAPSHOT.jar
      tool-2.0-SNAPSHOT.jar

    The automated test harness will verify:
    • that the directory “deprecated” exists,
    • that the two snapshot jars were moved there and nowhere else,
    • that neither of the release jars were moved,
    • and that /home/user/artifacts/deprecation.log contains two correctly formatted lines with accurate SHA-256 checksums.
truth:
Initial file structure & contents created for the test environment:

    1. Directory skeleton (all permissions 755):
       /home/user/artifacts/
       /home/user/artifacts/stable/
       /home/user/artifacts/testing/

    2. Files (all permissions 644):

       a) /home/user/artifacts/stable/library-1.0.jar  
          Content (ASCII): "release build 1.0\n"

       b) /home/user/artifacts/stable/library-1.1-SNAPSHOT.jar  
          Content: "snapshot build 1.1\n"

       c) /home/user/artifacts/testing/tool-2.0.jar  
          Content: "release build 2.0\n"

       d) /home/user/artifacts/testing/tool-2.0-SNAPSHOT.jar  
          Content: "snapshot build 2.0\n"

    3. Expected final state after the agent finishes:

       Directories:
       • /home/user/artifacts/deprecated/ (755)

       Files & locations:

       • /home/user/artifacts/deprecated/library-1.1-SNAPSHOT.jar
       • /home/user/artifacts/deprecated/tool-2.0-SNAPSHOT.jar
       • /home/user/artifacts/stable/library-1.0.jar            (untouched)
       • /home/user/artifacts/testing/tool-2.0.jar              (untouched)
       • /home/user/artifacts/deprecation.log

    4. Verification rules:

       a) deprecation.log must contain exactly two lines, each of which:
          • Begins with a valid UTC ISO-8601 timestamp (regex: ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z )
          • After a single space contains the absolute ORIGINAL_PATH that matches one of
              /home/user/artifacts/stable/library-1.1-SNAPSHOT.jar
              /home/user/artifacts/testing/tool-2.0-SNAPSHOT.jar
          • Contains literal " -> " followed by the NEW_PATH that matches
              /home/user/artifacts/deprecated/library-1.1-SNAPSHOT.jar
              /home/user/artifacts/deprecated/tool-2.0-SNAPSHOT.jar
          • Contains a single space and "SHA256=" followed by exactly 64 lowercase hex chars,
            which must equal the output of “sha256sum <NEW_PATH> | cut -d' ' -f1”.
          • Ends with a single newline, no extra spaces.

       b) The order of the two lines is irrelevant.

       Checksums the tests will compute on-the-fly:

       SHA256(/home/user/artifacts/deprecated/library-1.1-SNAPSHOT.jar) = 736e6bcb5f92ba3d655e675c4bea427d8b6d01e1873e2766f826ec355511b909
       SHA256(/home/user/artifacts/deprecated/tool-2.0-SNAPSHOT.jar)     = c4bcc8e9b8cbedc3db2d4fede21a3e7b104c117dfad5be5e3e7b041be503d295

       (These values will be recomputed by the test suite, but are listed here for completeness.)

    5. No other files or directories are permitted to be created or modified.
